preview_text:
The model favors Netherlands to win at 70%, with Tunisia assigned only an 8% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points is narrow by the model's reckoning, though a draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 22%.

The spread between Netherlands and Tunisia is substantial. The model's 61-percentage-point gap between the favorite and underdog suggests a heavily one-sided match in expectation, with draws plausible but Netherlands the clear dominant scenario across the probability distribution.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge on Tunisia's chances. Bookmakers assign the underdog 15%, roughly double the model's 8%, while Netherlands support drops to 63%. Polymarket prices fall between the two, at 13% for Tunisia and 65% for Netherlands. The disagreement centers on underdog win probability; the draw estimates remain stable across all three sources at 22%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate.
